title: Sample Claude Architecture Foundations Study Path

source.notes: Structured, paraphrased from the local PDF guide so site content and question metadata can stay independent of exam UI logic.

exam.audience: Solution architects and practitioners who design, implement, and evaluate production Claude applications.
exam.purpose: Validate practical judgment across Claude Code, Claude Agent SDK, Claude API, Model Context Protocol, agentic design, context management, prompt engineering, and reliability tradeoffs.
exam.questionFormat: Scenario-based multiple choice. Each question has one correct answer and three plausible distractors.

scoring.unansweredPolicy: Unanswered questions are scored incorrect. There is no penalty for guessing.

practiceBank.weightingMethod: Largest remainder allocation from source domain percentages for a 60-question sample exam.

candidateExperience: Builds agentic applications with the Claude Agent SDK, including multi-agent orchestration, subagent delegation, tools, and hooks.
Configures Claude Code for team workflows with CLAUDE.md, Agent Skills, MCP server integrations, plan mode, and CI usage.
Designs MCP tools and resources with clear interfaces, structured errors, and useful boundaries.
Engineers prompts for structured output using schemas, few-shot examples, extraction patterns, and validation loops.
Manages context windows across long conversations, large documents, codebase exploration, and multi-agent handoffs.
Chooses reliability, escalation, and human-review patterns for production Claude systems.

domains.label: Agentic Architecture & Orchestration
domains.description: Design model-driven agent loops, coordinator-subagent systems, handoffs, hooks, decomposition, and session state.

taskStatements.title: Design and implement agentic loops for autonomous task execution
taskStatements.summary: Use stop reasons and tool results to drive the loop rather than brittle natural-language completion checks.
knowledge: Agent loop lifecycle from Claude request to tool execution to returned tool result.
How tool results become part of conversation history for the next reasoning step.
Difference between model-driven tool decisions and preconfigured decision trees.
skills: Continue on tool_use and terminate on end_turn.
Append tool results between iterations.
Avoid natural-language completion heuristics or arbitrary caps as the main stop mechanism.

taskStatements.title: Orchestrate multi-agent systems with coordinator-subagent patterns
taskStatements.summary: Use a coordinator as the routing, aggregation, and recovery hub for isolated specialist agents.
knowledge: Hub-and-spoke coordinator architecture.
Subagents do not automatically inherit coordinator context.
Coordinator responsibilities for decomposition, delegation, aggregation, and gap handling.
skills: Select subagents dynamically based on the problem.
Partition scope to reduce duplicated or missing coverage.
Route subagent communication through the coordinator for observability and recovery.

taskStatements.title: Configure subagent invocation, context passing, and spawning
taskStatements.summary: Invoke subagents with Task, explicit context, structured metadata, and role-specific tool restrictions.
knowledge: The Task tool is required for subagent spawning and must be allowed to the coordinator.
Subagent prompts must include the context they need.
Agent definitions include descriptions, prompts, and allowed tool sets.
Forking can explore divergent paths from a shared baseline.
skills: Pass prior findings directly in subagent prompts.
Use structured data to preserve content and attribution metadata.
Spawn parallel subagents with multiple Task calls in one response.
Prompt coordinators with goals and quality criteria instead of rigid scripts.

taskStatements.title: Implement multi-step workflows with enforcement and handoff patterns
taskStatements.summary: Use deterministic gates for critical workflow order and structured summaries for escalations.
knowledge: Programmatic enforcement is different from prompt guidance.
Critical business rules need deterministic compliance.
Handoffs should include root cause, customer or case state, and recommended actions.
skills: Block downstream tools until prerequisites are satisfied.
Decompose multi-concern requests before synthesizing a response.
Compile structured escalation handoffs for humans without transcript access.

taskStatements.title: Apply Agent SDK hooks for tool call interception and data normalization
taskStatements.summary: Use hooks when compliance, normalization, or policy checks must happen reliably outside model discretion.
knowledge: PostToolUse hooks can transform results before model processing.
Outgoing tool-call hooks can block non-compliant actions.
Hooks provide deterministic guarantees where prompts only influence behavior probabilistically.
skills: Normalize timestamps, status codes, and heterogeneous tool payloads.
Block policy-violating tool calls and route to safer workflows.
Choose hooks over prompts for guaranteed business-rule compliance.

taskStatements.title: Design task decomposition strategies for complex workflows
taskStatements.summary: Match decomposition style to task shape: fixed chains for predictable reviews, adaptive plans for open-ended investigations.
knowledge: Fixed sequential pipelines versus dynamic adaptive decomposition.
Prompt chaining for focused review passes.
Adaptive investigation plans based on intermediate findings.
skills: Use prompt chaining for predictable multi-aspect reviews.
Split large reviews into local per-file passes and cross-file integration passes.
Map open-ended work before creating a prioritized adaptive plan.

taskStatements.title: Manage session state, resumption, and forking
taskStatements.summary: Resume or fork sessions deliberately, and avoid stale tool results by injecting current summaries when needed.
knowledge: Named session resumption with --resume.
fork_session for independent branches from a shared baseline.
Resumed sessions need awareness of changed files or stale results.
Fresh sessions with summaries can be safer than stale resumption.
skills: Resume named sessions for valid ongoing investigations.
Fork sessions for parallel strategy comparison.
Choose resume versus fresh summary based on staleness.
Tell resumed sessions exactly what changed.

domains.label: Tool Design & MCP Integration
domains.description: Design reliable tools, MCP integrations, structured errors, scoped tool access, and built-in tool usage.

taskStatements.title: Design effective tool interfaces with clear descriptions and boundaries
taskStatements.summary: Tool selection depends heavily on descriptions, names, examples, boundaries, and non-overlapping contracts.
knowledge: Tool descriptions are a primary selection signal.
Inputs, examples, edge cases, and boundaries improve selection reliability.
Ambiguous or overlapping descriptions cause misrouting.
Prompt wording can bias tool selection.
skills: Write differentiated tool descriptions.
Rename or split tools when capabilities overlap.
Review prompts for wording that undermines tool descriptions.

taskStatements.title: Implement structured error responses for MCP tools
taskStatements.summary: Return actionable error categories, retry metadata, and user-safe explanations so agents can recover correctly.
knowledge: MCP isError flag pattern.
Transient, validation, business, and permission error categories.
Generic failures prevent useful recovery.
Retryability must be explicit.
skills: Return errorCategory, isRetryable, and readable descriptions.
Mark business-rule errors as non-retryable with customer-safe explanations.
Recover locally from transient failures where possible.
Distinguish access failure from valid empty results.

taskStatements.title: Distribute tools appropriately across agents and configure tool choice
taskStatements.summary: Limit each agent to the tools needed for its role and use tool_choice when a tool call must happen.
knowledge: Too many tools degrade model selection reliability.
Agents misuse tools outside their specialization.
Scoped tool access improves reliability.
tool_choice supports auto, any, and forced tool selection.
skills: Restrict each subagent to role-appropriate tools.
Replace generic tools with constrained alternatives.
Provide scoped cross-role tools for frequent simple needs.
Use forced tool_choice or any when the workflow requires a tool call.

taskStatements.title: Integrate MCP servers into Claude Code and agent workflows
taskStatements.summary: Use project and user MCP scopes, environment expansion, resource catalogs, and clear descriptions to make MCP tools usable.
knowledge: Project-level .mcp.json versus user-level ~/.claude.json.
Environment variable expansion for credentials.
Configured MCP servers are discovered together at connection time.
MCP resources expose catalogs that reduce exploratory calls.
skills: Configure shared MCP servers in project scope.
Keep personal or experimental servers in user scope.
Improve MCP descriptions so agents pick them over weaker built-ins.
Expose catalogs as MCP resources.

taskStatements.title: Select and apply built-in tools effectively
taskStatements.summary: Choose Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, and Glob based on file search, content search, and edit requirements.
knowledge: Grep searches file contents.
Glob matches file paths and names.
Read and Write handle full-file operations.
Edit needs unique text anchors and may need fallback.
skills: Use Grep to find callers, imports, and error messages.
Use Glob for file patterns such as tests.
Use Read then Write when Edit cannot match uniquely.
Build codebase understanding incrementally from search to focused reads.

domains.label: Claude Code Configuration & Workflows
domains.description: Configure Claude Code memory, rules, skills, commands, plan mode, iterative workflows, and CI behavior.

taskStatements.title: Configure CLAUDE.md files with hierarchy, scoping, and modular organization
taskStatements.summary: Place shared instructions at project scope, keep personal instructions user-scoped, and modularize large memory files.
knowledge: User, project, and directory CLAUDE.md hierarchy.
User-level settings are not shared through version control.
@import keeps CLAUDE.md modular.
.claude/rules can organize topic-specific files.
skills: Diagnose hierarchy issues across teammates.
Use imports for relevant standards files.
Split large instruction files into focused rule files.
Use /memory to inspect loaded memory.

taskStatements.title: Create and configure custom slash commands and skills
taskStatements.summary: Use repository-scoped commands for team workflows and skills for on-demand, optionally isolated workflows.
knowledge: Project commands live under .claude/commands/ and are version-controlled.
User commands live under ~/.claude/commands/.
Skills use SKILL.md frontmatter including context, allowed tools, and argument hints.
Personal skill variants should avoid affecting teammates.
skills: Create shared slash commands in the repository.
Use context: fork to isolate verbose or exploratory skills.
Restrict skill tools with allowed-tools.
Add argument-hint for required parameters.
Choose skills for on-demand workflows and CLAUDE.md for always-loaded standards.

taskStatements.title: Apply path-specific rules for conditional convention loading
taskStatements.summary: Use path-scoped rules and glob patterns to load only the conventions relevant to the file being edited.
knowledge: .claude/rules files can include YAML paths fields.
Path-scoped rules reduce irrelevant context.
Glob rules work better than directory instructions when conventions span directories.
skills: Create rule files with path globs.
Apply conventions by file type regardless of directory.
Choose path-specific rules when files are distributed across the codebase.

taskStatements.title: Determine when to use plan mode vs direct execution
taskStatements.summary: Use plan mode for broad, architectural, or uncertain work; direct execution for simple, well-scoped changes.
knowledge: Plan mode supports exploration and design before changes.
Direct execution fits clear, small changes.
Explore subagents can isolate verbose discovery.
skills: Select plan mode for large-scale or multi-file architectural tasks.
Select direct execution for narrow fixes.
Use Explore for context-heavy discovery.
Combine plan mode for investigation with direct implementation.

taskStatements.title: Apply iterative refinement techniques for progressive improvement
taskStatements.summary: Use examples, tests, interview patterns, and targeted iteration to converge on reliable implementation behavior.
knowledge: Concrete input/output examples clarify transformations.
Test-driven iteration guides improvement with failures.
Interview patterns surface requirements before building.
Interacting issues may need one combined message; independent issues can be handled sequentially.
skills: Provide 2-3 examples for inconsistent transformations.
Write tests before implementation and iterate from failures.
Ask design questions in unfamiliar domains.
Group interacting problems when fixes depend on each other.

taskStatements.title: Integrate Claude Code into CI/CD pipelines
taskStatements.summary: Run Claude Code non-interactively, request structured JSON, avoid duplicate review output, and isolate review context.
knowledge: -p or --print runs Claude Code in non-interactive mode.
--output-format json and --json-schema enforce structured CI output.
CLAUDE.md provides project review and testing context.
Independent review sessions work better than self-review from the generation session.
skills: Use -p in CI to avoid hangs.
Use JSON schema output for machine-posted findings.
Include prior findings when rerunning reviews to avoid duplicates.
Provide existing tests and standards so generated tests are useful.

domains.label: Prompt Engineering & Structured Output
domains.description: Improve precision, use few-shot prompting, enforce schema output, validate retries, batch work, and design review architectures.

taskStatements.title: Design prompts with explicit criteria to improve precision and reduce false positives
taskStatements.summary: Replace vague instructions with categorical criteria, examples, and severity definitions.
knowledge: Explicit criteria work better than vague conservatism.
False positives undermine developer trust.
Severity should be defined with concrete examples.
skills: Define report versus skip categories.
Disable noisy categories while improving prompts.
Use concrete examples for severity classification.

taskStatements.title: Apply few-shot prompting to improve output consistency and quality
taskStatements.summary: Use targeted examples to demonstrate format, judgment, ambiguous cases, and extraction behavior.
knowledge: Few-shot examples are strong for consistent actionable output.
Examples teach ambiguous-case handling.
Examples help generalize judgment rather than match fixed cases.
Examples reduce hallucination in extraction tasks.
skills: Create 2-4 targeted examples.
Show the desired output format.
Distinguish acceptable patterns from real issues.
Demonstrate extraction across varied document formats.

taskStatements.title: Enforce structured output using tool use and JSON schemas
taskStatements.summary: Use tool_use with schemas and appropriate tool_choice settings to guarantee JSON syntax while still validating semantics.
knowledge: Tool use with JSON schemas eliminates JSON syntax errors.
tool_choice auto, any, and forced selection have different guarantees.
Schemas do not prevent semantic errors.
Required, optional, enum, other, and nullable fields shape extraction behavior.
skills: Define extraction tools with JSON schemas.
Use tool_choice any or forced tool calls when needed.
Make fields optional or nullable when sources may omit them.
Use extensible enums and normalization instructions.

taskStatements.title: Implement validation, retry, and feedback loops for extraction quality
taskStatements.summary: Use specific validation errors for retries and know when retries cannot recover absent source information.
knowledge: Retries work better with specific error feedback.
Retries cannot invent absent source information reliably.
Detected patterns support false-positive analysis.
Semantic validation differs from schema syntax validation.
skills: Retry with original document, failed extraction, and validation errors.
Identify retryable versus non-retryable failures.
Capture detected_pattern for feedback analysis.
Use self-correction fields such as calculated totals and conflict flags.

taskStatements.title: Design efficient batch processing strategies
taskStatements.summary: Use Message Batches for non-blocking workloads and synchronous calls where latency is required.
knowledge: Message Batches offer lower cost with up to 24-hour processing.
Batch is suitable for non-blocking work, not blocking checks.
Batch does not support multi-turn tool calls within one request.
custom_id correlates requests and responses.
skills: Match API approach to latency needs.
Calculate submission frequency against SLA windows.
Resubmit failed documents by custom_id.
Refine prompts on samples before large batch runs.

taskStatements.title: Design multi-instance and multi-pass review architectures
taskStatements.summary: Separate generation from review and split large reviews into focused passes to reduce self-review bias and attention dilution.
knowledge: Self-review is limited by retained reasoning context.
Independent review instances catch issues the generator may miss.
Multi-pass review reduces attention dilution.
skills: Use a second independent Claude instance for review.
Split local file review from cross-file integration review.
Use confidence reporting only as part of calibrated routing.

domains.label: Context Management & Reliability
domains.description: Preserve facts, manage long context, escalate appropriately, propagate errors, explore codebases, calibrate review, and keep provenance.

taskStatements.title: Manage conversation context to preserve critical information across long interactions
taskStatements.summary: Persist key facts outside lossy summaries and trim verbose tool output before it overwhelms context.
knowledge: Progressive summaries can blur amounts, dates, and commitments.
Lost-in-the-middle effects hide middle input details.
Verbose tool results can consume context without adding value.
Complete conversation history helps coherence.
skills: Maintain structured case facts.
Persist multi-issue state separately from summaries.
Trim tool results to relevant fields.
Place summaries early and use explicit section headers.

taskStatements.title: Design effective escalation and ambiguity resolution patterns
taskStatements.summary: Escalate on explicit human requests, policy gaps, no-progress states, and ambiguous identity rather than sentiment or self-confidence alone.
knowledge: Escalation triggers include human requests, policy gaps, and inability to progress.
Straightforward issues may be resolved even when a customer is frustrated unless they request a human.
Sentiment and self-reported confidence are weak proxies for complexity.
Multiple matches require clarification.
skills: Add escalation criteria with examples.
Honor explicit human-agent requests immediately.
Acknowledge frustration while resolving straightforward cases.
Ask for identifiers rather than guessing among multiple matches.

taskStatements.title: Implement error propagation strategies across multi-agent systems
taskStatements.summary: Propagate structured failure context and partial results so coordinators can recover without hiding or overreacting to errors.
knowledge: Structured error context enables recovery decisions.
Access failures differ from valid empty results.
Generic errors hide useful recovery context.
Suppressing errors and terminating on single failures are both anti-patterns.
skills: Return failure type, attempted query, partial results, and alternatives.
Distinguish empty successful results from failures.
Recover locally before escalating.
Annotate synthesis coverage gaps.

taskStatements.title: Manage context effectively in large codebase exploration
taskStatements.summary: Use scratchpads, subagents, summaries, manifests, and compaction to keep long codebase investigations reliable.
knowledge: Extended sessions can drift toward generic answers.
Scratchpads persist key findings.
Subagents isolate verbose exploration.
Structured state exports support crash recovery.
skills: Spawn focused exploration subagents.
Maintain scratchpad files with key findings.
Summarize before spawning the next exploration phase.
Load recovery manifests on resume.
Use /compact when verbose discovery fills context.

taskStatements.title: Design human review workflows and confidence calibration
taskStatements.summary: Validate automation by segment and calibrate confidence thresholds with labeled data before reducing human review.
knowledge: Aggregate accuracy can hide weak segments.
Stratified sampling detects errors in high-confidence outputs.
Field-level confidence needs labeled validation calibration.
Accuracy should be checked by document type and field.
skills: Sample high-confidence outputs across segments.
Analyze accuracy by document type and field.
Calibrate confidence thresholds with labeled sets.
Route low confidence or ambiguous records to human review.

taskStatements.title: Preserve information provenance and handle uncertainty in multi-source synthesis
taskStatements.summary: Keep claim-source mappings, temporal metadata, and uncertainty labels through synthesis instead of flattening evidence.
knowledge: Summarization can lose source attribution.
Structured claim-source mappings must survive synthesis.
Conflicting credible statistics should be annotated, not arbitrarily chosen.
Temporal data needs publication or collection dates.
skills: Require claim-source mappings from subagents.
Separate established findings from contested ones.
Pass conflicting values to the coordinator with attribution.
Require publication dates for temporal interpretation.
Render different content types in appropriate formats.

scenarios.label: Customer Support Resolution Agent
scenarios.summary: Build a Claude Agent SDK support agent for ambiguous returns, billing disputes, and account issues with MCP tools such as get_customer, lookup_order, process_refund, and escalate_to_human.

scenarios.label: Code Generation with Claude Code
scenarios.summary: Use Claude Code for generation, refactoring, debugging, documentation, custom slash commands, CLAUDE.md configuration, and plan-mode decisions.

scenarios.label: Multi-Agent Research System
scenarios.summary: Coordinate web search, document analysis, synthesis, and report generation subagents to produce comprehensive cited reports.

scenarios.label: Developer Productivity with Claude
scenarios.summary: Build developer tooling for unfamiliar codebases, legacy systems, boilerplate, repetitive tasks, built-in tools, and MCP integrations.

scenarios.label: Claude Code for Continuous Integration
scenarios.summary: Integrate Claude Code into CI/CD for automated code review, test generation, and pull-request feedback with actionable low-noise prompts.

scenarios.label: Structured Data Extraction
scenarios.summary: Extract structured data from unstructured documents using JSON schemas, validation, retries, edge-case handling, and downstream integration.

sampleQuestions.promptFocus: The support agent sometimes skips customer verification before order lookup or refund operations.
sampleQuestions.learningPoint: Use a programmatic prerequisite gate when critical business logic requires deterministic tool order.

sampleQuestions.promptFocus: Minimal tool descriptions cause get_customer and lookup_order to be confused for order-related requests.
sampleQuestions.learningPoint: Improve tool descriptions with input formats, examples, edge cases, and boundaries before adding heavier routing infrastructure.

sampleQuestions.promptFocus: Escalation calibration is inverted: simple cases escalate while policy-exception cases are handled autonomously.
sampleQuestions.learningPoint: Add explicit escalation criteria with few-shot examples instead of relying on sentiment or model self-confidence.

sampleQuestions.promptFocus: A team-wide custom /review slash command must be available to every developer from the repository.
sampleQuestions.learningPoint: Project-scoped slash commands belong in the repository under .claude/commands/.

sampleQuestions.promptFocus: A monolith-to-microservices restructure spans many files and requires architectural decisions.
sampleQuestions.learningPoint: Use plan mode for large architectural changes that require exploration before edits.

sampleQuestions.promptFocus: Different code areas and test files need conventions loaded automatically by file path.
sampleQuestions.learningPoint: Use .claude/rules/ files with path globs for conventions that span directories or file types.

sampleQuestions.promptFocus: A report about creative industries misses music, writing, and film because the coordinator decomposed too narrowly.
sampleQuestions.learningPoint: When subagents complete assigned tasks but coverage is incomplete, inspect the coordinator's decomposition and scope partitioning.

sampleQuestions.promptFocus: A web search subagent times out and the coordinator needs enough information to recover intelligently.
sampleQuestions.learningPoint: Return structured failure type, attempted query, partial results, and alternatives rather than a generic unavailable status.

sampleQuestions.promptFocus: The synthesis agent needs frequent simple fact verification without round-tripping through full web-search delegation.
sampleQuestions.learningPoint: Give the synthesis agent a scoped verify_fact tool for common simple checks while routing complex work through the coordinator.

sampleQuestions.promptFocus: A CI job hangs because Claude Code is waiting for interactive input.
sampleQuestions.learningPoint: Use claude -p or --print for non-interactive Claude Code execution in automated pipelines.

sampleQuestions.promptFocus: A team wants Message Batches cost savings for both pre-merge checks and overnight technical debt reports.
sampleQuestions.learningPoint: Use batch processing for latency-tolerant overnight work, but keep real-time calls for blocking pre-merge checks.

sampleQuestions.promptFocus: A single-pass review across 14 files gives uneven and contradictory feedback.
sampleQuestions.learningPoint: Split large reviews into per-file local passes and a separate cross-file integration pass.

preparationExercises.label: Build a Multi-Tool Agent with Escalation Logic
preparationExercises.objective: Practice agentic loops, tool integration, structured errors, hooks, escalation, and multi-concern handling.
steps: Define 3-4 MCP tools with differentiated descriptions and boundary conditions.
Implement an agentic loop driven by stop_reason values.
Add structured errors with category, retryability, and readable descriptions.
Use a hook to enforce a business rule and route to escalation.
Test multi-concern messages and unified response synthesis.

preparationExercises.label: Configure Claude Code for a Team Development Workflow
preparationExercises.objective: Practice CLAUDE.md hierarchy, custom commands, path rules, skills, and MCP integration.
steps: Create project-level CLAUDE.md standards and verify team-shared behavior.
Create .claude/rules files with path globs for code areas and tests.
Create a project skill with context: fork and allowed-tools restrictions.
Configure project and personal MCP servers with credential expansion.
Compare plan mode and direct execution on tasks of different complexity.

preparationExercises.label: Build a Structured Data Extraction Pipeline
preparationExercises.objective: Practice JSON schemas, tool_use, validation-retry loops, batches, and human review routing.
steps: Define extraction schemas with required, optional, enum, other, and nullable fields.
Retry failed validations with the failed extraction and specific validation errors.
Add few-shot examples for varied document formats.
Submit and recover batch work using custom_id correlation.
Route low-confidence or ambiguous extractions to human review and segment accuracy.

preparationExercises.label: Design and Debug a Multi-Agent Research Pipeline
preparationExercises.objective: Practice subagent orchestration, explicit context passing, error propagation, and provenance-preserving synthesis.
steps: Build a coordinator with at least two subagents and explicit Task access.
Run parallel subagents through multiple Task calls in one response.
Require structured claim, source, excerpt, and date metadata.
Simulate subagent timeout and propagate structured recovery context.
Test conflicting source data and preserve both values with attribution.

technologiesAndConcepts: Built-in tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob
outOfScope: Fine-tuning Claude models or training custom models
Claude API authentication, billing, or account management
Detailed implementation of specific languages or frameworks beyond tool and schema configuration
Deploying or hosting MCP server infrastructure
Claude internal architecture, training process, or model weights
Constitutional AI, RLHF, or safety training methods
Embedding models or vector database implementation details
Streaming API or server-sent event implementation
Rate limits, quotas, or API pricing calculations
OAuth, API key rotation, or authentication protocol details
Performance benchmarking or model comparison metrics
Prompt caching implementation details beyond knowing it exists
Token counting algorithms or tokenization specifics
preparationRecommendations: Build a Claude Agent SDK agent with a complete tool-calling loop, errors, sessions, subagents, and context passing.
Configure Claude Code for a real project with CLAUDE.md hierarchy, path rules, skills, and an MCP server.
Design and test MCP tools with differentiated descriptions and structured errors.
Build a structured extraction pipeline with schemas, retries, nullable fields, and Message Batches.
Practice few-shot prompts, explicit review criteria, and multi-pass review architecture.
Study context management patterns such as fact extraction, scratchpads, and subagent delegation.
Review escalation and human-in-the-loop patterns for policy gaps, explicit human requests, and calibrated confidence routing.
Complete the practice exam and study explanations after each answer.
